# AgentCamp — Full Page Content

> A 5-week program where you build your own AI agent — one that remembers, connects to your tools, handles payments, and proves its work.

By [Frutero](https://frutero.club) · In Spanish · 50 spots · Starts May 11, 2026

---

## Hero

**Intensive program · 5 weeks · In Spanish**

Stop experimenting with AI. Start operating with agents.

A 5-week program where you build your own AI agent — one that remembers, connects to your tools, handles payments, and proves its work.

$300 USD first 20 spots · $450 USD regular · 50 spots · Starts May 11

- [Apply: I want my spot](https://tally.so/r/aQ2D0b)

"The orchestration-first approach changed how I think about automation. Now everything has control."

**Ana López** — Content Creator @ Independent
"I thought I needed a developer. Agentcamp showed me I don't."

---

## Proven Track Record

This isn't the first time we've done this.

AgentCamp has run in multiple formats — virtual in English and Spanish, in-person in New York and Denver — with hundreds of builders and measurable results.

What the numbers say:

- **356** participants in the first edition (virtual, English and Spanish)
- **143** participants in the in-person bootcamps (NYC + Denver)
- **100+** agents integrated with payments, social media and custom workflows
- **6** hackathon winners — projects born in AgentCamp

---

## Pain Points

You've tried ChatGPT, Claude, Copilot and 17 other tools. You're still doing everything yourself.

The problem isn't AI. It's the gap between opening a tool and having a system that works for you.

You've built prompts that work once and break the next day. You've duct-taped Zapier to GPT to Google Sheets and called it "automation." You've spent more time configuring tools than doing the work they were supposed to handle.

AgentCamp exists to close that gap.

---

## Transformation

It's not a course. It's a program to build agents that operate.

### Build
Your agent, its architecture, and its skills.

### Connect
Your tools, your files, your identity.

### Prove
Real workflows, real metrics, real track record.

---

## How It Works

5 weeks · 2 live sessions per week · 1 artifact per week

- **Week 1 — Activate**: Your first agent running from day one.
- **Week 2 — Architect**: Understand how your agent thinks and teach it new skills.
- **Week 3 — Connect**: Your agent joins the real world with its own identity.
- **Week 4 — Automate**: Workflows that run without you — and transact.
- **Week 5 — Launch**: Public demo with real evidence and a verified track record.

---

## Curriculum

5 weeks. 10 sessions. 5 real artifacts.

### Week 1 — From prompt to first agent
In the first session you set up your personal agent and put it to work on a real task. In the second you learn to instruct it like a professional: prompts, context, and the art of directing intelligence.

**What you learn:** What separates an agent from a chatbot. How to provision an agent in Studio. Prompt structure, context layers, system instructions.

**What you build:** A personal agent active in Studio, solving a real task from your actual work.

### Week 2 — Agent architecture
We open the engine. You understand how bootstrap files, memory, context, and skills work — the pieces that separate a real agent from a chatbot in disguise.

**What you learn:** Bootstrap files, persistent vs. ephemeral memory, context layers, skills as composable capabilities.

**What you build:** An agent with its own architecture, configured memory, and functional skills you created yourself.

### Week 3 — Integrations, tools, and identity
Your agent leaves the sandbox. It connects to your email, calendar, files, your stack — and activates its own persistent identity. From this moment, everything it does is recorded in its track record.

**What you learn:** Tool calling, native integrations (Gmail, Calendar, GitHub, files), what it means for an agent to have its own identity, how reputation starts building automatically.

**What you build:** An agent connected to your real tools, with its own identity, inside an operational workspace. Track record starts accumulating.

### Week 4 — Workflows and agent economics
You design workflows that run without you. You define how your agent charges for its outputs or pays for services — Studio handles the rails. From isolated tasks to complete processes that transact.

**What you learn:** Workflow design (triggers, multi-step, conditionals, error recovery, human-in-the-loop), how to declare payment rules, when an agent should pay vs. charge, spend controls.

**What you build:** A multi-step workflow solving a real case, with a working economic layer — your agent pays, charges, or both.

### Week 5 — Iteration, reputation, and Demo Day
You review the track record your agent has been building since Week 3. You iterate with real evidence. We close with Demo Day: you present your capstone in front of sponsors, investors, and the community.

**What you learn:** Basic evals, logging, tracing, common failure patterns, evidence-based iteration, how to read and present your agent's verified track record.

**What you build:** A capstone with recorded demo, real metrics, documented case, and a verified track record of your agent's work.

---

## What You Build With

AgentCamp runs on production-grade infrastructure — the same tools serious teams use to ship real agents.

| Layer | Description | Partner |
|-------|-------------|---------|
| Agent environment | Where you build. No code, no terminal, launch your first agent in 3 minutes. Identity, payment rails, and reputation tracking built in. | Agent Platform Partner |
| Agent architecture | How your agent thinks, remembers, and learns new skills. Open-source runtime. | Architecture Partner |
| Intelligence | Your agent's brain. The models that power reasoning and generation. | LLM Partner(s) |
| Identity | Your agent gets its own persistent identity — recognizably yours across sessions. | Identity Partner |
| Payments | Your agent can charge, pay, and transact. You define the rules, the rails handle the rest. | Payments Partner(s) |
| Reputation | Your agent builds a verified track record of real work — proof, not promises. | Reputation Partner |

Part of the Frutero ecosystem.

Want to power the next generation of agent operators? → [Partner with us](/sponsorship-deck)

---

## What You Leave With

- An agent that works for you — with memory, identity, and real integrations to your tools
- A workflow running real work without you — that can charge, pay, and prove what it's done
- A capstone at Demo Day — recorded demo, real metrics, verified track record, documented case

Everything. For every graduate. No tiers, no "if you get there."

## Who AgentCamp Is For

### It's for you if:
- You run real work and you're tired of doing it all yourself
- You've tried AI tools — you know there's something there, but nothing sticks
- You want to leave with a system, not a certificate

### Not for you if:
- You want to watch, not build
- You're collecting courses — we don't give refunds for showing up and not shipping
- You need hand-holding through every step — this is intensive, not passive

---

## Manifesto

Serious agents don't live in a sandbox.

You build agents that remember, transact, prove their work, and run when you're not watching. That's the standard here.

---

## Demo Day · June 7

The program closes with a public presentation of capstones in front of community, sponsors, and ecosystem guests.

Demo Day is not ceremony. It's proof.

You leave with a recorded demo, a documented case, real metrics, and a verified track record — your place in the first generation of Agent Operators.

- **Early bird: $300 USD** (first 20 spots)
- **Regular: $450 USD**
- Pay with credit or debit card via Stripe.

### Includes:
- 5 weeks of live program (10 sessions) + recordings
- Access to Godínez.AI Studio (5 weeks + 1 month post-program)
- Mentorship with the Frutero ecosystem and partners
- Templates and playbooks
- Agent Operator certification
- Access to the Frutero community

### Logistics:
- Capacity: 50 spots
- Starts: May 11, 2026
- Registration closes: May 10, 23:59 CDMX

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Do I need to know how to code?**
No. You build inside Godínez.AI Studio — no terminal, no code, no complicated setup. If you can use your daily tools, you have more than enough.

**How is this different from other AI courses?**
Most courses teach you to prompt. You leave with notes. Here, you leave with an agent that has its own memory, connects to your tools, handles payments, and builds a track record of real work. That's not a chatbot — that's infrastructure.

**How much time do I need per week?**
4–6 hours: 2 live sessions plus building time. If you fall behind, you have recordings and mentorship.

**How technical is the program?**
Technical enough to build a serious agent. Guided enough so you don't get lost. Studio handles the infrastructure — you focus on building.

**What do I build by the end?**
A working agent with its own identity, connected to your tools, running workflows that can transact, with a verified track record — presented at Demo Day with a recorded demo and real metrics.

**What is Godínez.AI Studio?**
The environment where you build. It gives you agent architecture, memory, skills, identity, payment rails, and reputation tracking — so you focus on building agents that work, not configuring infrastructure. No code. Launch your first agent in 3 minutes.

**What happens after the program?**
Graduation to the alumni community. Access to the Frutero ecosystem. Invitation to the public launch of Godínez.AI on June 17. Graduates with strong capstones may be eligible for post-program bounties and grants from ecosystem partners.

**What if I have questions before signing up?**
Talk to the team → WhatsApp

# Sponsorship Deck — Full Content

> Partner with the first agent operator program in Latin America. Your infrastructure powers the agents they build.

AgentCamp 2026 · 50 operators across Latin America, building on your stack.

---

## The Opportunity

Category leadership in the emerging LATAM AI agent operator market.

- **50** operators per cohort — Professionals, founders, and business owners who decide what tools their teams adopt
- **100%** powered by your infra — Your technology runs under the hood — every agent uses your stack through the platform
- **1st** in LATAM · in Spanish — No comparable program exists. You define the category, not compete in it

---

## What Is AgentCamp (Sponsor View)

A 5-week practical program where participants build real AI agents.

### Week 1 — Activate
"Your first agent, running from day one"
- Agent environment setup & first run
- Prompt engineering fundamentals
- Context and instruction design
→ **Project:** First Working Agent (input: your use case + AI-assisted setup → output: an active agent solving a real task)

### Week 2 — Architect
"How your agent thinks and learns new skills"
- Bootstrap, memory, and context layers
- Skills framework with OpenClaw
- The difference between a chatbot and a real agent
→ **Project:** Agent with Architecture (input: functional skills + memory → output: an agent with its own architecture)

### Week 3 — Connect
"Your agent touches the real world"
- Integration with real tools and files
- Persistent identity setup
- Workspace and environment configuration
→ **Project:** Live Integrations (input: tools, files, identity layer → output: real integrations + active identity)

### Week 4 — Automate
"Workflows that run without you"
- Workflow design and automation
- Agent economics and payment rails
- Human-in-the-loop patterns
→ **Project:** Autonomous Workflow (input: real case + economic capability → output: a workflow solving real work)

### Week 5 — Launch
"Iterate, prove, and present"
- Evaluation and iteration
- Reputation and public track record
- Demo Day preparation
→ **Project:** Capstone at Demo Day (input: recorded demo + real metrics → output: documented case + public reputation)

Every graduate leaves with: a working agent with its own identity, workflows that transact, and a verified track record.

---

## The Audience

50 high-intent operators who will actively build on your infrastructure.

- **Operators & decision-makers** — Managers, consultants, founders, business owners — the people who decide what tools their teams adopt.
- **AI-active, not AI-curious** — They've tried ChatGPT and Claude. They're frustrated nothing sticks. They're ready to invest in learning.
- **Spanish-speaking LATAM** — The fastest-growing market for AI adoption. No comparable program exists in Spanish.
- **Downstream influence** — These aren't junior developers — they bring tools back to their teams, companies, and clients.

---

## How Sponsorship Works

Your technology powers what they build. Participants don't configure your infrastructure directly — the platform abstracts that. Every agent they build runs on your stack. Your brand is associated with the capabilities that make their agents real.

| Layer | When | What |
|-------|------|------|
| Identity | Week 3 | Every agent gets persistent identity — your wallet infrastructure powers it |
| Payments | Week 4 | Every workflow gets an economic layer — your payment rails handle settlement |
| Reputation | Week 5 | Agent track records verified at Demo Day — your protocol is the proof layer |
| Intelligence | W1–5 | Your models power every agent built in the program — 50 agents running daily |
| Environment | W1–5 | The platform where participants live for 5 weeks — your brand is the workspace |

---

## Sponsorship Tiers

### Founding Partner — $5,000 USD
2–3 slots available · Category exclusive

- Category exclusivity — only one identity partner, one payments partner, one intelligence partner
- Your technology powers the agent infrastructure every participant uses throughout the program
- Named in curriculum materials and sessions — participants learn what your tech enables
- Demo Day presenting sponsor — your brand introduces the capstone presentations
- Co-marketing rights: case studies, testimonials, video content from Demo Day
- Logo on all materials: landing page, emails, recordings, certificates
- Post-program: graduates continue on the platform, which continues running on your stack
- First right of refusal for future cohorts

### Infrastructure Partner — $2,500 USD
3–5 slots available

- Named in curriculum — your technology referenced as infrastructure powering agent capabilities
- Demo Day access — attend, network, evaluate capstones
- Logo on landing page, emails, and recordings
- Community access — direct channel to AgentCamp graduates
- Co-marketing: shared social content and participant highlights
- Post-program: graduates continue on the platform running on your stack

### Startup Partner — $1,000 USD
Open slots · Credits and in-kind support do not count toward the fee

- Named as supporting partner in program materials
- Demo Day access — attend, network, evaluate capstones
- Logo on landing page and recordings
- Community access — direct channel to AgentCamp graduates
- Co-marketing: shared social content and participant highlights
- Optional: provide API credits or tooling for participants (in addition to fee, not instead of)

### Community Partner — No fee
For communities, DAOs, collectives, and ecosystem groups — not companies

- Cross-promote AgentCamp to your community — we promote yours to ours
- Co-host a session, AMA, or workshop during the program
- Logo on landing page as community partner
- Demo Day invitation for your community leads
- Shared content: social co-promotion, recaps, highlights

*This tier is for builder communities, DAOs, developer collectives, and ecosystem groups that want to cross-pollinate audiences. Companies and startups should see the paid tiers.*

---

## Tier Comparison

| Feature | Founding ($5,000) | Infra ($2,500) | Startup ($1,000) | Community (Free) |
|---------|:-:|:-:|:-:|:-:|
| Category exclusivity | ✓ | – | – | – |
| Powers agent infrastructure | ✓ | ✓ | – | – |
| Named in curriculum | ✓ | ✓ | – | – |
| Demo Day presenting sponsor | ✓ | – | – | – |
| Demo Day access | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Logo on all materials | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Co-marketing rights | Full | Social | Social | Cross-promo |
| Post-program platform | ✓ | ✓ | – | – |
| Community cross-promotion | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| First refusal future cohorts | ✓ | – | – | – |

---

## Timeline

- **Now** — Partner commitments confirmed
- **April 25** — Partner logos and materials finalized
- **May 11** — Cohort 1 starts — 50 participants begin building
- **May–Jun** — 5 weeks of live sessions — your tech powers the infrastructure
- **June 7** — Demo Day — capstone presentations, sponsor judges, public event
- **June 17** — Godínez.AI public launch — graduates arrive with working agents
- **June+** — Post-program: case studies, testimonials, pipeline activation

---

## Why Cohort 1

Founding partners shape the program.

- **First-mover in LATAM AI agents** — No comparable program exists in Spanish. You're defining the category, not competing in it.
- **Curriculum integration, not afterthought** — Cohort 1 partners co-design how their technology is taught. Later cohorts inherit your structure.
- **50 operators → organic growth** — Each graduate becomes an advocate in their team, company, and network. 50 operators seed hundreds of users.
- **Godínez.AI launch alignment** — Cohort 1 graduates arrive on launch day (June 17) with working agents. Your infrastructure is what they're built on.
